title: marsilea schelpeana
intro: Marsilea schelpeana, also known as the ladder fern or water clover, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Marsileaceae. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Marsilea schelpeana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and treat various infections. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions to respiratory problems.
